THE WEAPON OF THE BELIEVER

A treatise on the status and etiquette of dua in Islam

by Abu Ammaar Yasir Qadhi

C — Du a The Weapon of the Believer

A treatise on the status and etiquette of du‘ a in Islam

Abu Ammaar Yasir Qadhi

44 > SAG BAC Cr7 AS oer .

'o) S) yan And your Lord has stated: Make du’ a to Me, I will (of

a surety) respond to you. Verily, those who are too arrogant to worship Me will enter the Fire of Hell, humiliated.

Du ‘a is (the essence of) worship. Prophetic hadith

dua and the seeking of protection from Allah are like weapons, but the sharpness of a weapon is not sufficient for it to cause effect, for the person that handles it also plays a role. So whenever the weapon is a perfect one, having no flaw in it, and the forearm is strong, and there are no preventing factors, then it will cause an effect on the enemy...

€ a Du a The Weapon of the Believer A treatise on the status and etiquette of du‘ a in Islam

3. Tawassul by Mentioning the State One is in 4. Tawassul through Good Deeds 5. Tawassul Through Mentioning the Effect of the Du‘a

7. The Du‘a of Yanus 8. The Du‘a of Animals 9. The Du‘a of the Prophet (#%) Against a Person

6. The Sixth Hadith

All praise is due to Allah. We praise Him, seek His aid, and ask His forgiveness. We also seek refuge in Allah from the evil of our souls, and from our impious deeds. Indeed, whoever Allah guides, none can misguide, and whoever He misguides, there is none that can guide him. I bear witness and testify that there is no deity worthy of worship except Allah, all glory be to Him, and IJ bear witness and testify that Muhammad is His final Messenger, and His perfect worshipper.

Verily, man has been created for a noble and great purpose ~ in fact, the most noble and dignified goal that can exist — and that is that he may worship Allah alone, without any partners. Allah has said in the Quran:

Gy ott By ak us

And I have only created jinn and mankind to worship Me.

And the greatest and most noble form of worship is du’ a. It is a plea from the very heart of a believer directed towards Allah — the Hearer of all things’, the Knower of all secrets. It is a confession that emanates from the heart of a believer that he is weak and helpless, that he cannot achieve anything without Allah’s help and aid. _ It is an implicit affirmation of every single Name and Attribute of Allah, for it affirms that Allah is the Creator, the Sustainer, the Controller of all Affairs, the Hearer, the Seer, the Merciful, the Great, the All-Powerful, the Ever-Capable. The concept of dua entails

' Sarah al-Dhariyar, 56. 2 Tt should be remembered that Allah’s Names and Attributes are only in Arabic, and that the translations used in this treatise — and in all English books — convey only a small fraction of the real Arabic meaning of the word. Therefore, these translations should not, and cannot, take the place

of the actual Arabic Names and Attributes of Allah.

complete submission to Allah, and perfect recognition of His right to be worshipped. It also consigns man to his proper status — a poor, created being, who has no control of any matter by himself, but rather he is the one that is controlled. It is a confirmation and attestation from the worshipper that he is in need of his Creator at every instance, and that Allah is independent of any need from him. It shows that man is in a dire state of poverty towards his Lord, and that he cannot live without Him in any circumstance. In fact, his need for his Lord is more than his need for food, drink and air, for it is his Lord that provides him with all this and more. In particular, he is in need of continual guidance from His Lord, and this is .the most important of all his needs, and the most noble of all his wants,

Du ‘ais the essence of worship. This can be seen by imagining the condition of one making du‘ a — a worshipper repenting from his sins, humiliated in front of His Lord, in a state of fear, submitting himself to the will of Allah, earnestly desiring Allah’s rewards, raising his hands, turning to Allah with the best of hopes from Him, exemplifying the statement of Allah:

BIE Dp e45] Or SOE CAC AL “Verily, they used to hasten to do good deeds, and they used to

make du’ 4 to Us with hope and fear, and used to humble themselves before Us.”

Such a person makes du‘a, keeping in mind the promise that Allah has given him, hopeful of achieving that promise:

4a 5ib ear cieans 3M)

3 Sarah al-Anbiya, 90.

And your Lord has said: “Call upon me, and I will answer you!” Verily, those who are (too) arrogant to worship Allah will enter Hell, humiliated!‘

So when one visualises such a person, and the state that he will

So the entire concept of worship, the purpose for which mankind was created, can infact, be summarised in one simple act: that of du’ a.

Dua is a noble, spiritual form of worship which allows the created to appreciate the majesty and eminence of the Creator. This is because the worshipper turns to his Lord at his time of need, after all other types of aid have been cut off, and all other sources have been despaired of. He turns to his Lord to obtain from Him contentment, peace and serenity, and he finds Beneficence which will never be found from other sources. And how can this not happen, when he is turning to the Lord of all lords, and the King of all kings, the One Who is Self-Sufficient from all wants, and Who is Praised at all times?

So with the noble status of this act of worship in mind, it becomes obligatory on every single believer, without exception, to become familiar with this great concept of du’a. So many questions that abound in one’s mind must be answered. What is dua? What are its blessings and its excellence? What is the proper etiquette that one must follow while making du‘ a? Why is someone’s dua responded to, and someone else’s not? And how can one increase one’s chances of getting one’s dua@ accepted? And what are the factors that prevent one’s du'@ from being accepted? Also, if everything is already predestined, then what is the purpose of making

* Sarah Ghafir, 60.

Authentic, narrated by Ahmad and the four Sunans, and others, from Nu‘man ibn Bashir, as reported in Sahih al-Jami # 3407.

du 4, for surely, if it is already written, it will happen whether the dua is made or not, and if it is not written, then no matter how much du‘ one does, it will never occur?

All these questions, and more , are answered in the short treatise in your hands. Although there are many books in English about the concept and etiquette of du'a, in this author’s humble opinion, none of them have done justice to this topic. Most only deal with some of the etiquette of dz‘ a, and none actually discuss the importance and status of dw‘ a in a Muslim’s life. Therefore, I felt a dire need for such a book in the English language, and compiled this short, yet insha-Allah comprehensive, treatise, in which I pray that the reader will find essential information that is needed by every Muslim with regard to this greatest act of worship. I have deliberately not elaborated unnecessarily on many advanced points or difficult concepts, nor have I diverged to discuss obscure tangents, for the purpose of this book is not so that a person masters the many facets of du’ @, but rather that the reader obtains practical benefit, by applying what he has read in his daily life. This work is meant for the layman who wishes to learn more about dua@ so that he can worship Allah properly; it is not for the scholar. Therefore, the chapter sections have deliberately been left short, for I have restricted myself to quoting some @ya¢ and ahadith, and the statements of scholars, along with short explanations where appropriate.
content_id:
SENJATA ORANG BERIMAN

Sebuah risalah tentang kedudukan dan adab du'a dalam Islam

oleh Abu Ammaar Yasir Qadhi

C — Du a The Weapon of the Believer

Sebuah risalah tentang kedudukan dan adab du'a dalam Islam

Abu Ammaar Yasir Qadhi

44 > SAG BAC Cr7 AS oer .

'o) S) yan Dan Tuhanmu telah berfirman: Berdoalah kepada-Ku, niscaya Aku akan (pasti) mengabulkannya. Sesungguhnya, orang-orang yang terlalu sombong untuk menyembah-Ku akan masuk ke dalam Neraka Jahannam dalam keadaan hina.

Du'a adalah (inti dari) ibadah. Hadith Nabi

du'a dan permohonan perlindungan kepada Allah bagaikan senjata, namun ketajaman sebuah senjata saja tidak cukup untuk menimbulkan efek, karena orang yang menggunakannya pun berperan. Maka apabila senjatanya sempurna, tidak ada cacat padanya, dan lengan yang menggunakannya kuat, serta tidak ada faktor-faktor yang menghalangi, barulah senjata itu akan memberikan efek pada musuh...

€ a Du'a Senjata Orang Beriman Sebuah risalah tentang kedudukan dan adab du'a dalam Islam

1. Berkenaan dengan Hakikat Du'a 2. Berkenaan dengan Dzat yang Dimintai 3. Berkenaan dengan Orang yang Berdoa

20. Du'a adalah Tanda Persaudaraan

1. Menyadari bahwa Hanya Allah yang Mengabulkan Du'a. 2. Ikhlas dalam Du'a Hanya kepada Allah Semata

Bab 10: Faktor-Faktor yang Menghalangi Terkabulnya Du'a .. 155

1. Rezeki yang Haram w.eeeecseccecseeeseerssstesasssceesesenescessseeeseettseeeaesees 155

3. Meninggalkan Nasihat kepada Orang Lain.. 4. Tergesa-gesa

dan Seorang Laki-laki yang Memberikan Hartanya kepada Orang yang Bodoh u.... te teseseseeeeeees 160

9. Sesuatu yang Dibenci Terkadang Mendatangkan Kebaikan

10. Hal Itu Mendorong Orang Beriman untuk Merenungi Dirinya ..

2. Mengharapkan Kematian dalam Keadaan yang Berat

3. Mendoakan dan Mendoakan Keburukan bagi Non-Muslim ....

4. Meminta Orang Shaleh untuk Melakukan Du'a v.sesceceseeccsveoee 182

3. Tawassul dengan Menyebutkan Keadaan yang Sedang Dialami 4. Tawassul melalui Amal Shaleh 5. Tawassul dengan Menyebutkan Pengaruh dari Du'a

7. Du'a Yunus 8. Du'a Hewan-Hewan 9. Du'a Nabi (#%) terhadap Seseorang

6. Hadith Keenam

Segala puji bagi Allah. Kami memuji-Nya, memohon pertolongan-Nya, dan memohon ampunan-Nya. Kami juga berlindung kepada Allah dari kejahatan jiwa-jiwa kami, dan dari perbuatan-perbuatan buruk kami. Sesungguhnya, siapa pun yang Allah beri petunjuk, tidak ada yang dapat menyesatkannya, dan siapa pun yang Dia sesatkan, tidak ada yang dapat memberi petunjuk kepadanya. Aku bersaksi dan menegaskan bahwa tidak ada ilah yang berhak disembah selain Allah, segala kemuliaan bagi-Nya, dan aku bersaksi dan menegaskan bahwa Muhammad adalah utusan-Nya yang terakhir, dan hamba-Nya yang sempurna.

Sesungguhnya, manusia diciptakan untuk tujuan yang mulia dan agung — bahkan, tujuan yang paling mulia dan bermartabat yang dapat ada — yaitu agar ia dapat menyembah Allah semata, tanpa menyekutukan-Nya dengan siapa pun. Allah telah berfirman dalam Quran:

Gy ott By ak us

Dan tidaklah Aku ciptakan jin dan manusia kecuali untuk menyembah-Ku.

Dan bentuk ibadah yang paling agung dan mulia adalah du'a. Ia adalah permohonan dari lubuk hati seorang mukmin yang ditujukan kepada Allah — Yang Maha Mendengar segala sesuatu, Yang Maha Mengetahui segala rahasia. Ia adalah sebuah pengakuan yang memancar dari hati seorang mukmin bahwa ia lemah dan tak berdaya, bahwa ia tidak dapat mencapai apa pun tanpa pertolongan dan bantuan Allah. Ia adalah penegasan tersirat atas setiap Nama dan Sifat Allah, karena ia menegaskan bahwa Allah adalah Sang Pencipta, Sang Pemberi Rezeki, Sang Pengatur segala Urusan, Yang Maha Mendengar, Yang Maha Melihat, Yang Maha Pengasih, Yang Mahaagung, Yang Mahakuasa, Yang Maha Mampu. Konsep du'a mengandung

' Surah al-Dzariyat, 56. 2 Perlu diingat bahwa Nama-Nama dan Sifat-Sifat Allah hanya dalam bahasa Arab, dan bahwa terjemahan yang digunakan dalam risalah ini — dan dalam semua buku berbahasa Inggris — hanya menyampaikan sebagian kecil dari makna Arab yang sebenarnya dari kata tersebut. Oleh karena itu, terjemahan-terjemahan ini tidak boleh, dan tidak dapat, menggantikan Nama-Nama dan Sifat-Sifat Allah dalam bahasa Arab yang sebenarnya.

kepasrahan total kepada Allah, dan pengakuan sempurna atas hak-Nya untuk disembah. Ia juga menempatkan manusia pada statusnya yang sebenarnya — makhluk yang lemah dan diciptakan, yang tidak memiliki kendali atas urusan apapun dengan sendirinya, melainkan ia adalah pihak yang dikendalikan. Ia adalah sebuah konfirmasi dan pembuktian dari sang hamba bahwa ia membutuhkan Penciptanya di setiap saat, dan bahwa Allah tidak membutuhkan apa pun dari dirinya. Hal ini menunjukkan bahwa manusia berada dalam kondisi kefakiran yang sangat mendalam terhadap Tuhannya, dan bahwa ia tidak dapat hidup tanpa-Nya dalam keadaan apapun. Bahkan, kebutuhannya kepada Tuhannya melebihi kebutuhannya terhadap makanan, minuman, dan udara, karena Tuhannya-lah yang menyediakan semua itu dan lebih banyak lagi baginya. Secara khusus, ia membutuhkan bimbingan yang berkesinambungan dari Tuhannya, dan inilah yang paling penting dari semua kebutuhannya, dan yang paling mulia dari semua keinginannya.

Du'a adalah inti dari ibadah. Hal ini dapat dipahami dengan membayangkan kondisi orang yang berdoa — seorang hamba yang bertaubat dari dosa-dosanya, dalam keadaan hina di hadapan Tuhannya, dalam kondisi takut, menyerahkan dirinya kepada kehendak Allah, dengan sungguh-sungguh mengharapkan pahala Allah, mengangkat kedua tangannya, menghadap Allah dengan harapan terbaik dari-Nya, mewujudkan firman Allah:

BIE Dp e45] Or SOE CAC AL "Sesungguhnya, mereka biasa bersegera dalam melakukan kebaikan, dan mereka biasa berdoa kepada Kami dengan penuh harap dan takut, dan mereka biasa merendahkan diri kepada Kami."

Orang seperti itu berdoa, dengan mengingat janji yang telah Allah berikan kepadanya, penuh harap untuk mewujudkan janji tersebut:

4a 5ib ear cieans 3M)

3 Surah al-Anbiya, 90.

Dan Tuhanmu telah berfirman: "Berdoalah kepada-Ku, niscaya Aku akan mengabulkannya!" Sesungguhnya, orang-orang yang (terlalu) sombong untuk menyembah Allah akan masuk Neraka dalam keadaan hina!

Maka ketika seseorang membayangkan orang seperti itu, dan kondisi yang akan ia alami

Maka seluruh konsep ibadah, tujuan untuk itulah manusia diciptakan, pada hakikatnya dapat dirangkum dalam satu tindakan sederhana: yaitu du'a.

Du'a adalah bentuk ibadah yang mulia dan bersifat rohani yang memungkinkan makhluk untuk menghargai keagungan dan kemuliaan Sang Pencipta. Hal ini karena sang hamba berpaling kepada Tuhannya di saat ia membutuhkan, setelah semua bentuk bantuan lainnya telah terputus, dan semua sumber lainnya telah putus harapan. Ia berpaling kepada Tuhannya untuk memperoleh darinya kepuasan, kedamaian, dan ketenangan, dan ia menemukan Kemurahan Hati yang tidak akan pernah ditemukan dari sumber-sumber lain. Dan bagaimana hal ini tidak terjadi, ketika ia sedang berpaling kepada Tuhan dari segala tuhan, dan Raja dari segala raja, Dzat Yang Maha Kaya dari segala kebutuhan, dan Yang Dipuji di setiap saat?

Maka dengan mempertimbangkan kedudukan mulia dari ibadah ini, menjadi wajib bagi setiap mukmin, tanpa terkecuali, untuk memahami dengan baik konsep du'a yang agung ini. Banyak sekali pertanyaan yang memenuhi pikiran seseorang yang harus dijawab. Apakah du'a itu? Apa keistimewaan dan keutamaannya? Apa adab yang benar yang harus diikuti saat berdoa? Mengapa du'a seseorang dikabulkan, sedangkan du'a orang lain tidak? Dan bagaimana seseorang dapat meningkatkan peluang agar du'anya diterima? Dan apa saja faktor-faktor yang menghalangi terkabulnya du'a seseorang? Juga, jika segala sesuatu sudah ditakdirkan, maka apa gunanya berdoa

* Surah Ghafir, 60.

Shahih, diriwayatkan oleh Ahmad dan empat Sunan, serta lainnya, dari Nu'man ibn Bashir, sebagaimana tercantum dalam Sahih al-Jami # 3407.

du 4, karena sesungguhnya, jika memang sudah tertulis, maka hal itu akan terjadi baik du'a dipanjatkan maupun tidak, dan jika tidak tertulis, maka seberapa pun banyaknya du'a yang dipanjatkan, hal itu tidak akan pernah terjadi?

Semua pertanyaan ini, dan lebih banyak lagi, dijawab dalam risalah singkat yang ada di tangan Anda. Meskipun banyak buku dalam bahasa Inggris yang membahas konsep dan adab du'a, menurut pendapat rendah hati penulis, tidak satu pun dari buku-buku tersebut yang telah memberikan keadilan kepada topik ini. Kebanyakan hanya membahas sebagian dari adab du'a, dan tidak satu pun yang benar-benar mendiskusikan pentingnya dan kedudukan du'a dalam kehidupan seorang Muslim. Oleh karena itu, saya merasakan kebutuhan yang sangat mendesak untuk hadirnya buku semacam ini dalam bahasa Inggris, dan menyusun risalah singkat namun insya-Allah komprehensif ini, yang di dalamnya saya berdoa agar pembaca menemukan informasi penting yang dibutuhkan oleh setiap Muslim berkenaan dengan ibadah yang paling agung ini. Saya sengaja tidak menguraikan secara berlebihan banyak poin-poin lanjutan atau konsep yang sulit, dan tidak pula menyimpang untuk membahas hal-hal yang tidak relevan, karena tujuan buku ini bukanlah agar seseorang menguasai berbagai segi du'a, melainkan agar pembaca memperoleh manfaat praktis dengan menerapkan apa yang telah dibacanya dalam kehidupan sehari-hari. Karya ini ditujukan bagi orang awam yang ingin mengetahui lebih banyak tentang du'a agar ia dapat beribadah kepada Allah dengan benar; bukan untuk para ulama. Oleh karena itu, bagian-bagian bab sengaja dibuat singkat, karena saya membatasi diri dengan mengutip beberapa ayat dan ahadith, serta pernyataan para ulama, beserta penjelasan singkat di tempat yang sesuai.

To Request a Pious Person to Perform a Du'Gl v.sesceceseeccsveoee 182 3. Tawassul by Mentioning the State One is in 4. Tawassul through الخير Deeds 5. Tawassul Through Mentioning the Effect of the Du‘a 7. The Du‘a of Yanus 8. The Du‘a of Animals 9. The Du‘a of النبي ﷺ (#%) Against a Person 6. The سادساً الحديث All praise is بسبب الله. We praise Him, seek His aid, and ask His المغفرة. We أيضاً seek refuge in الله from the الشر of our النفوس, and from our impious deeds. Indeed, whoever الله guides, none can misguide, and whoever He misguides, there is none that can guide him. I bear witness and testify that there is no deity worthy of worship except الله, all glory be to Him, and IJ bear witness and testify that محمد ﷺ is His final Messenger, and His perfect worshipper. Verily, man has been created for a noble and great purpose ~ in fact, the most noble and dignified goal that can exist — and that is that he may worship الله alone, without any partners. الله has said in the القرآن الكريم: Gy ott By ak us And I have only created الجن and mankind للعبادة Me. And the greatest and most noble form of worship is du’ a. It is a plea from the very القلب of a المؤمن directed towards الله — the Hearer of all things’, the Knower of all secrets. It is a confession that emanates from the القلب of a المؤمن that he is weak and helpless, that he cannot achieve anything without الله’s help and aid. _ It is an implicit affirmation of every single Name and Attribute of الله, for it affirms that الله is the Creator, the Sustainer, the Controller of all Affairs, the Hearer, the Seer, the Merciful, the Great, the All-Powerful, the Ever-Capable. The concept of الدعاء entails ' Sarah al-Dhariyar, 56. 2 Tt should be remembered that الله’s Names and Attributes are only in Arabic, and that the translations used in this treatise — and in all English الكتب — convey only a small fraction of the real Arabic meaning of the word. لذلك, these translations should not, and cannot, take the place of the actual Arabic Names and Attributes of الله. complete submission to الله, and perfect recognition of His الحق to be worshipped. It أيضاً consigns man to his proper status — a poor, created being, who has no control of any matter by himself, but rather he is the one that is controlled. It is a confirmation and attestation from the worshipper that he is in need of his Creator at every instance, and that الله is independent of any need from him. It shows that man is in a dire state of الفقر towards his Lord, and that he cannot live without Him in any circumstance. In fact, his need for his Lord is more than his need for food, drink and air, for it is his Lord that provides him with all this and more. وبالأخص, he is in need of continual الهداية from His Lord, and this is .the most المهم of all his needs, and the most noble of all his wants, Du ‘ais the essence of worship. This can be seen by imagining the condition of one making du‘ a — a worshipper repenting from his الذنوب, humiliated in front of His Lord, in a state of الخوف, submitting himself to the will of الله, earnestly desiring الله’s rewards, raising his hands, turning to الله with the best of hopes from Him, exemplifying the statement of الله: BIE Dp e45] Or SOE CAC AL “Verily, they used to hasten to do الخير deeds, and they used to make du’ 4 to Us with الأمل and الخوف, and used to humble themselves قبل Us.” Such a person makes du‘a, keeping in العقل the promise that الله has given him, hopeful of achieving that promise: 4a 5ib ear cieans 3M) 3 Sarah al-Anbiya, 90. And your Lord has said: “Call upon me, and I will answer you!” Verily, those who are (أيضاً) arrogant للعبادة الله will enter النار, humiliated!‘ لذا عندما one visualises such a person, and the state that he will لذا the entire concept of worship, the purpose for which mankind was created, can infact, be summarised in one simple act: that of du’ a. الدعاء is a noble, spiritual form of worship which allows the created to appreciate the majesty and eminence of the Creator. This is لأن the worshipper turns to his Lord at his time of need, بعد all other types of aid have been cut off, and all other sources have been despaired of. He turns to his Lord to obtain from Him contentment, peace and serenity, and he finds Beneficence which will never be found from other sources. And how can this not happen, عندما he is turning to the Lord of all lords, and the King of all kings, the One Who is Self-Sufficient from all wants, and Who is Praised at all times? لذا with the noble status of this act of worship in العقل, it becomes obligatory on every single المؤمن, without exception, to become familiar with this great concept of du’a. لذا many questions that abound in one’s العقل must be answered. What is الدعاء? What are its البركات and its excellence? What is the proper الآداب that على المرء follow بينما making du‘ a? Why is someone’s الدعاء responded to, and someone else’s not? And how can one increase one’s chances of getting one’s الدعاء@ accepted? And what are the factors that prevent one’s du'@ from being accepted? أيضاً, إذا everything is already predestined, then what is the purpose of making * Sarah Ghafir, 60. Authentic, narrated by أحمد and the four Sunans, and others, from Nu‘man ibn Bashir, as reported in صحيح al-Jami # 3407. du 4, for surely, إذا it is already written, it will happen whether the الدعاء is made or not, and إذا it is not written, then no matter how much du‘ one does, it will never occur? All these questions, and more , are answered in the short treatise in your hands. وإن كان there are many الكتب in English about the concept and الآداب of الدعاء, in this author’s humble opinion, none of them have done العدل to this topic. Most only deal with some of the الآداب of dz‘ a, and none actually discuss the importance and status of dw‘ a in a مسلم’s الحياة. لذلك, I felt a dire need for such a الكتاب in the English language, and compiled this short, yet insha-الله comprehensive, treatise, in which I pray that the reader will find الجوهري information that is needed by every مسلم فيما يتعلق بـ this greatest act of worship. I have deliberately not elaborated unnecessarily on many advanced points or difficult concepts, nor have I diverged to discuss obscure tangents, for the purpose of this الكتاب is not لذا that a person masters the many facets of du’ @, but rather that the reader obtains practical benefit, by applying what he has read in his daily الحياة. This work is meant for the layman who wishes to learn more about الدعاء@ لذا that he can worship الله properly; it is not for the scholar. لذلك, the الباب sections have deliberately been left short, for I have restricted myself to quoting some @ya¢ and الأحاديث, and the statements of العلماء, along with short explanations where appropriate.
